title: Notification state management: extend tts.local.md with notify/speak fields
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-session voice selection via /voice
description: When running multiple Claude Code sessions with TTS, each session should be able to set its own voice so the user can distinguish them audibly. /voice should support selecting a voice name (e.g. /voice matilda, /voice aria). Voice selection needs to persist for the session and be passed through to all speak tool calls. Consider: storing in tts.local.md, passing as a default to the MCP server, voice list/preview commands.

title: Screen reader mode: vocalize any plugin tool output
description:
Generic voice mode for punt-tts. When voice is enabled, a PostToolUse hook vocalizes tool output from any MCP tool in the session — not just tts tools. Acts like a screen reader: it speaks whatever text appears, with no awareness of the tool semantics.

Mechanism: PostToolUse hook matches all MCP tool results (or a configurable allowlist). Reads ~/.claude/tts.local.md for voice_enabled: true. Extracts text content from the tool result. Returns additionalContext instructing Claude to call synthesize with the extracted text, ephemeral=True, auto_play=True.

Scope: Generic — no per-sender voices, no selective vocalization, no creative control over pacing or tone. Uses the session default voice for everything.

Relationship to biff voice mode: Complementary, not competing. Biff voice mode (biff-k8u in punt-labs/biff) is biff-native and controls the audio experience for messaging. Screen reader mode is punt-tts-native and provides baseline vocalization for any tool output. When both are active, biff voice mode handles biff tools (richer experience) and screen reader mode handles everything else. Biff tools should be excluded from the screen reader allowlist to avoid double-speaking.

Config: Allowlist/blocklist in ~/.claude/tts.local.md or tts settings to control which tools get vocalized.

title: /speak command: toggle voice vs chime y|n
close_reason: Closed

title: install.sh: handle SSH auth failure gracefully
description: GitHub issue #8. install.sh runs 'claude plugin install tts@punt-labs' which clones via SSH. Fails for HTTPS-only users (no SSH keys). Fix: detect SSH connectivity, fall back to HTTPS git rewrite, clean up after. Affects any user without GitHub SSH keys configured (common on fresh Linux installs). See https://github.com/punt-labs/tts/issues/8 for suggested fix.
close_reason: PR #9 merged — SSH probe + HTTPS fallback in install.sh

title: Add /voice slash command to punt-tts plugin
description: The Claude Code plugin shell exists (plugin.json, MCP server entry) but has no custom slash commands. Add /voice on|off command that toggles voice mode via .claude/tts.local.md config. When voice is on, a PostToolUse hook auto-speaks synthesis results.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #1: session-start.sh with dev/prod namespace, voice.md + voice-dev.md commands

title: Rename MCP tools to fun, on-brand names
description: Rename the four MCP tools from clinical names to fun, voice/audio-themed names. Current names are technical and appear frequently in the UI: synthesize, synthesize_batch, synthesize_pair, synthesize_pair_batch. Follow the dungeon plugin pattern where tools were renamed from generic (load, save, delete) to thematic (recall, inscribe, obliterate). Naming should evoke the voice/audio domain — think about what a sound engineer, DJ, or voice actor would call these operations. Must update: server.py tool decorators, suppress-output.sh hook (tool name matching), tests, CLAUDE.md architecture table. Must coordinate with suppress-output.sh since it matches on tool_name.
close_reason: Merged PR #4 — renamed tools: speak, chorus, duet, ensemble

title: Remove debug vibe_mode output from stop hook block reason
description: The notify.sh stop hook includes 'vibe_mode: auto' in its block reason output, which leaks into the user-visible hook error display. The block reason should only contain the ♪ phrase. The vibe_mode data needs to be passed to the LLM some other way (e.g. structured metadata) or parsed out before display. Currently shows: 'Stop hook error: ♪ Voicing my closing remarks...\nvibe_mode: auto' — the second line is debug data that shouldn't be visible.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #26 — vibe data removed from block reason, instructions updated to read .tts/config.md

title: install.sh does not upgrade already-installed plugin to current version
description: After releasing v0.6.0, the installed plugin remained at v0.4.0. Running install.sh (via install-all.sh) updated the PyPI package and ran `claude plugin marketplace update punt-labs`, but did not upgrade the already-installed plugin. Had to manually run `claude plugin install tts --marketplace punt-labs` to get v0.6.0. The install.sh should detect when the installed plugin version is behind the marketplace version and reinstall it.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #28 — install.sh now detects existing plugin and runs claude plugin update instead of no-op install

title: Architecture B: log-tailing MCP server for /notify c continuous mode
description:
When notify=c, the MCP server spawns a background watcher thread that tails the active session JSONL. The watcher detects milestones from the event stream and triggers speech independently — no model involvement, no extra tokens, no compliance issues.

Milestone detection by pattern matching on JSONL entries:
- type=assistant with tool_use Bash containing git commit
- type=assistant with tool_use Bash containing pytest
- type=assistant with tool_use Write for new files
- type=assistant with tool_use Task for subagent completion

Key design decisions:
1. Session discovery: find active JSONL by most-recently-modified in project dir
2. Throttle: minimum N seconds between announcements
3. Config: reads .tts/config.md — only active when notify=c
4. Audio collision: must coordinate with stop hook and model-driven speak (depends on tts-clh)
5. Synthesis: uses existing provider pipeline
6. Respects speak=n chime mode

Current state: notify=c is accepted but behaves identically to notify=y. This bead makes c actually continuous.

title: Auto-vibe: detect session mood from hook signals
description: Accumulator-based auto-vibe: PostToolUse on Bash appends signals (test_pass, lint_fail, git_push_ok, etc.) to .tts/mood-signals. Stop hook scores signals and writes vibe_tags to config. SessionStart sets time-of-day baseline. Manual /vibe always overrides. Defaults to /vibe auto, supports /vibe off. Config writes via MCP (tts-mqa), not file tools. Panel note on vibe shifts (e.g. '♪ vibe shifted to [weary]').

title: Two-channel display output for MCP tools
description: Implement the two-channel display pattern (punt-kit/patterns/two-channel-display.md) for TTS MCP tool results. Current suppress-output.sh is functional but bland — 'Speaking: hello world...', 'Synthesized 3 items'. Follow biff's approach: unicode-formatted panel summaries with personality, and structured additionalContext for the model. Panel output should be compact, expressive, and max 80 columns. Consider emoji or unicode glyphs for visual identity (biff uses ▶). additionalContext should carry the full JSON result so the model can reason about paths, voices, languages without cluttering the panel.
close_reason: Merged PR #5 — two-channel display with ♪ prefix and voice/provider context

title: Fix double vibe tag prepend in speak tool output
description: When calling speak with vibe tags in the text (e.g. '[calm] Filed tts-e59...'), the result shows the tag doubled: '[calm] [calm] Filed tts-e59...'. The server or provider layer is prepending vibe_tags from config.md onto the text, but the caller is also including them. Either the server should not auto-prepend when tags are already present in the text, or the stop hook protocol should instruct the LLM not to include tags in the text (since set_config already sets them and the server prepends them).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #27 — _apply_vibe skips prepend when text already starts with a tag

title: Audio queue: play utterances sequentially instead of kill-previous
description:
The kill-previous fix (267c700) prevents overlapping audio but discards the interrupted utterance. All three playback paths (MCP _play_audio, stop hook chime, notification hook chime/voice) should enqueue audio instead, so every utterance plays to completion in FIFO order.

Possible approaches:
- A lightweight daemon/socket that accepts file paths and plays them sequentially
- A named pipe or Unix domain socket that all three paths write to
- A lock file with a wait loop (simplest but blocks the caller)

The queue must work across Python (server.py) and bash (hooks) and across independent Claude Code sessions.
close_reason: Implemented in v0.4.0 via flock-based playback serialization (playback.py). enqueue() + play_audio() provide FIFO ordering across Python and bash callers.

title: /notify command: toggle task notifications y|c|n
close_reason: Closed

title: Short commands (/recap, /say) not available — only namespaced /tts:recap works
close_reason: Fixed by uninstall/reinstall after v0.3.4 plugin name fix

title: Install should update plugin when already installed
description: When a user already has the tts plugin installed, running install.sh or tts install only checks claude plugin install which says 'already installed' and moves on. It never calls claude plugin update tts@punt-labs, so users stay stuck on their old version. The installer should: (1) try install, (2) if already installed, run update.
close_reason: PR #14 merged: installer now calls claude plugin update when already installed, plus --scope user fix from Bugbot review

title: Rename MCP server key from tts to vox
description: Rename the MCP server key in plugin.json from 'tts' to 'vox' so the display reads plugin:tts:vox instead of plugin:tts:tts. Breaking change: all allowed-tools references in commands change from mcp__plugin_tts_tts__* to mcp__plugin_tts_vox__*. Also update suppress-output.sh hook matcher, session-start.sh auto-allow patterns, and release-plugin.sh sed patterns.
close_reason: Closed

title: Notification hook: permission-prompt and idle-prompt audio alerts
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Claude Code plugin shell: /voice, hooks, config
description: Add plugin integration points following quarry pattern. plugin.json declaring MCP server. SessionStart hook (deploy commands, auto-allow permissions). PostToolUse suppress-output hook (hide JSON blobs). /voice on|off command. .claude/tts.local.md config (voice name, auto-speak triggers). Dev/prod namespace isolation (tts-dev / tts).
close_reason: Plugin shell shipped in v0.1.0 (phase 4): plugin.json, hooks, /voice, /say

title: Improve command help strings with exact input examples
description: Add argument-hint frontmatter to all tts commands (pattern from biff). Shows autocomplete-style hints in the slash command picker. Examples: /notify → 'y | c | n', /speak → 'y | n', /say → '<text>', /voice → 'on | off | status', /recap → (none, no args).
close_reason: Added argument-hint frontmatter to notify, speak, say, voice commands

title: Audio collision: prevent overlapping TTS utterances
description: Two problems: (1) Cross-session collision — multiple Claude Code sessions with TTS can speak simultaneously, talking over each other. Needs a shared lock or coordination layer. (2) Same-session double-fire — occasionally two utterances play from the same session for a single event. Root cause unknown — could be stop hook + notification hook both triggering, or a race in the loop guard. Need to reproduce and diagnose both paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: Move config from global ~/.claude/tts.local.md to per-project .tts/config.md
description: tts.local.md is at ~/.claude/ (global), so /notify, /speak, /voice affect ALL sessions across all projects. Should be per-project like biff's .biff/ directory. Move config to .tts/config.md in the repo root. The .tts/ directory already exists for ephemeral audio — add config.md alongside it. Update: state.sh (hooks reader), all commands that read/write config, session-start.sh, and .gitignore guidance.
close_reason: PR #11 merged — config moved to per-project .tts/config.md

title: Streaming TTS: use ElevenLabs stream API to reduce time-to-first-audio
description: Replace batch text_to_speech.convert() with text_to_speech.stream() for the speak MCP tool. The text is already known upfront so this is HTTP streaming (not WebSocket), but audio chunks start playing before full synthesis completes. Reduces perceived latency from ~1-2s to ~75ms with Flash v2.5 model. Does not change the API surface — callers still get an MP3 file back. The stream() method returns Iterator[bytes] which we write to disk as chunks arrive, then play. Optional: pipe chunks directly to afplay via stdin for true streaming playback (no temp file). This is a latency optimization, not a feature change.
close_reason: Released in v0.3.6

title: Investigate missing voice name in speak tool suppress-output display
description: The suppress-output.sh PostToolUse hook formats MCP tool results for the UI panel. When speak completes, it shows '♪ the voice has spoken' but should include the voice name (e.g. '♪ matilda said the piece'). The raw result JSON includes the voice field — the hook's formatting logic may be dropping it or using a fallback string. Compare with other tool outputs that do show voice names correctly.
close_reason: Fixed in 505ca71 — added result wrapper unwrapping to suppress-output.sh jq extraction

title: Stop hook: task-completion spoken notification
close_reason: Closed

title: Session mood via ElevenLabs audio tags
description:
New /mood command that sets an ElevenLabs eleven_v3 audio tag for the session. All TTS utterances embed the tag (e.g. [dramatic tone], [whisper], [excited], [tired]). Store in tts.local.md, inject into speak tool calls. Fun/viral feature — people sharing clips of their AI whispering code reviews or dramatically announcing releases. Only works with ElevenLabs eleven_v3 model.

Stretch: time-aware auto-mood. Session-start hook checks the clock and sets a default mood — normal during the day, [tired] after 10pm, [whisper] after midnight. The voice naturally adapts to late-night coding sessions without any user action.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #23: /vibe command with two-field model (vibe + vibe_tags), Claude-as-voice-director interpretation, ElevenLabs expressive tags. Auto-vibe tracked separately in tts-5eo.

title: Chime audio: bundled MP3 tones for /speak n mode
close_reason: Closed

title: Add hooks to punt-tts Claude Code plugin
description: The plugin.json exists but no hooks are implemented. Needs: SessionStart hook (deploy commands, auto-allow MCP permissions), PostToolUse hook (suppress raw JSON output from synthesis tools, auto-play audio). Follow quarry/biff plugin patterns.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #1: session-start.sh with dev/prod namespace, voice.md + voice-dev.md commands

title: Add ephemeral output mode for Claude Code sessions
description: Add ephemeral parameter to synthesize MCP tools. When ephemeral=true: write to .tts/ in project root (gitignored), delete previous files before each new synthesis, delete last file on session end (SessionEnd hook). When ephemeral=false (default): write to ~/tts-output/, persist files. Supports both voice-plugin use (ephemeral) and langlearn use (persistent).
close_reason: Ephemeral output mode shipped in v0.1.0 (phase 3)

title: Create punt-tts: extract general-purpose TTS from langlearn-tts
description: Fork langlearn-tts into punt-labs/tts. Strip langlearn-types dependency. Define own types locally (SynthesisRequest, SynthesisResult, TTSProvider). Add ephemeral parameter to synthesize (write to .tts/, auto-delete after playback). Default output dir ~/tts-output/. CLI command: tts. PyPI: punt-tts. All 3 providers (ElevenLabs, Polly, OpenAI), all 4 MCP tools (synthesize, batch, pair, pair-batch), doctor, install. Quality gates: ruff, mypy, pyright, pytest.
close_reason: v0.1.0 released to PyPI — source, tests, CI, release pipeline all complete

title: MCP tool for config writes instead of file-tool edits
description: Commands like /vibe, /voice, /notify, /speak all modify .tts/config.md using Claude's Read/Write/Edit file tools. This works but is inelegant — the user sees file operations in the panel for what should be a simple config toggle. Add an MCP tool (e.g. 'config' or 'set') that writes config fields server-side. Commands call the MCP tool instead of editing files directly. Benefits: cleaner panel output (no file-write noise), atomic config updates, single source of truth for config parsing (server already reads config for vibe_tags). The tool would accept field/value pairs and update .tts/config.md internally.

title: Submit punt-tts to Claude Code marketplace and Desktop extension directory
description: Build .mcpb Desktop Extension bundle. Submit to Claude Desktop extension directory. Submit plugin to Claude Code marketplace. Verify install flow: curl install.sh, tts doctor, /voice on.
close_reason: Marketplace installer shipped in v0.2.0. Plugin released to PyPI, tagged, GitHub release created.

title: Remove recap-dev and say-dev commands from prod plugin install
description: The production plugin install (via marketplace/tagged release) currently includes recap-dev.md and say-dev.md command files. These are dev-namespace commands meant only for local development testing (--plugin-dir .) and should be stripped by scripts/release-plugin.sh before tagging. Verify the release script removes *-dev.md commands and that the latest published release doesn't contain them.
close_reason: Not a bug: v0.6.0 prod install is clean (no dev commands). The recap-dev and say-dev commands appear only when running with --plugin-dir . (dev mode), which is by design. The release-plugin.sh script correctly strips *-dev.md commands before tagging.

title: Release CI: TestPyPI verification step fails after successful publish
description: The release workflow publishes to TestPyPI successfully, but the subsequent verification step (pip install from TestPyPI) reports failure. This has happened on multiple releases (v0.4.0, v0.5.0, v0.6.0 — all required manual workflow_dispatch reruns). Likely a race condition: TestPyPI index hasn't propagated the new version by the time the verify step runs. Investigate adding a retry/delay or switching to a polling check.

title: /recap command: on-demand spoken summary of last response
close_reason: Closed

title: Consolidate stop-hook vibe into single set_config call
description: Currently the stop-hook continuation requires two set_config calls before speak: one to set vibe_tags and one to clear vibe_signals. This creates visual noise (two ♪ → lines). The speak tool doesn't accept vibe tags as a parameter — it reads them from the config file via _apply_vibe(). Solution: add an optional vibe_tags parameter to speak (and chorus) that applies tags inline without a separate set_config call. The server can also clear vibe_signals internally when vibe_tags are provided this way. Result: stop continuation becomes a single speak call with vibe_tags included.

title: Permission notification phrase variation
description: notify-permission.sh currently uses fixed phrases: 'Needs your approval.' (permission_prompt) and 'Waiting for your input.' (idle_prompt). Hearing the exact same phrase every time is annoying and robotic. Add a pool of ~5-8 natural variations per notification type and randomly select one. Examples for permission_prompt: 'Hey, quick approval needed.', 'Need a green light here.', 'Got a question for you.', 'Your call on this one.' The variations should sound human and conversational, not corporate. Use bash RANDOM or shuf for selection. Keep the pool in the script itself (no external config).
close_reason: Merged PR #3 — phrase pools with Bash 3.2-compatible pick_random

title: Playful panel output: personify the voice
description: Change suppress-output.sh panel lines from factual metadata to playful personification. speak: '♪ matilda has spoken' instead of '♪ spoken — matilda (elevenlabs)'. Drop provider from display. Apply same pattern to duet/chorus/ensemble. Add phrase pool for variety like the stop hook.
close_reason: Personified panel output with phrase pools in suppress-output.sh
